description: Load Testing infrastructure with k6 and Admin Observability Page. Existing k6/ has 3 scripts (chat-fanout, feed-ranking, search). Wave 4 adds: a proper loadtest/ directory with smoke/load/stress scenarios, auth helpers, SLO definitions, README, plus a new admin panel observability page.
steps: Create loadtest/ directory structure at root
Create loadtest/k6/helpers/auth.js - helper to generate JWT tokens for authenticated requests (import crypto from k6, sign HS256 JWT with configurable secret)
Create loadtest/k6/helpers/config.js - shared config (BASE_URL from env, default thresholds, common headers)
Create loadtest/k6/scenarios/smoke.js - 1 VU, 10 iterations, verifies all critical endpoints respond 2xx (health, auth, search, inbox list)
Create loadtest/k6/scenarios/load.js - 50 VUs ramping over 5 min, measures p95 latency across core API endpoints (emails, messages, search, AI ask)
Create loadtest/k6/scenarios/stress.js - ramp to 200 VUs over 3 min, sustained 2 min, ramp down. Find breaking point with checks on error rate
Create loadtest/slo.json - SLO definitions (p95 < 200ms, p99 < 500ms, error_rate < 0.1%, throughput > 1000 rps)
Create loadtest/README.md - how to install k6, run each scenario, interpret results, SLO targets explanation

Create apps/admin/src/app/observability/page.tsx - admin observability page with: service health grid (green/yellow/red), latency percentiles chart (mock data for now, wired to /api/services endpoint), error tracking table, trace search form, service dependency visualization (simple DAG showing quantmail -> postgres, quantchat -> redis, etc.)
Create apps/admin/src/app/observability/components/ with TracesTable.tsx, LatencyChart.tsx, ServiceMap.tsx, ErrorTracker.tsx as client components
Update apps/admin/src/components/ sidebar/navigation to include Observability link
pnpm turbo typecheck && pnpm turbo build && pnpm turbo lint to verify
acceptance_criteria: loadtest/k6/scenarios/smoke.js is valid k6 ES6 module with options export and default function
loadtest/k6/scenarios/load.js defines 50 VU scenario with p95 threshold
loadtest/slo.json defines p95, p99, error_rate, throughput targets
Admin sidebar includes Observability navigation link
